title: FEMS injection scoring + graceful degradation under CPU/GPU load (folded WP-1-FEMS-Injection-Scoring-Graceful-Degradation)
goal: Implement the deterministic scoring formula for MemoryPack item ranking and a progressive retrieval pipeline that degrades gracefully under CPU/GPU load instead of failing hard latency gates. Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Injection-Scoring-Graceful-Degradation. Ports the injection scoring formula from repo governance and the progressive retrieval pattern from memory systems research.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/scoring.rs (InjectionScoringFormula + ScoreInputs types) + src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/progressive_retrieval.rs (ProgressiveRetriever + DegradationTier + DegradationReport types). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/injection_scoring_tests.rs + progressive_retrieval_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-141 (MemoryCapsule), MT-142 (TaskType + DegradationTier enum already defined there), MT-143 (CapsuleBuilder consumes scoring).
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Injection-Scoring-Graceful-Degradation preserved_intent: 'FEMS spec says "drop lowest priority items" on budget overflow but defines no scoring algorithm. The p95 500ms latency target is unrealistic under heavy load'. InjectionScoringFormula: deterministic combination of (a) importance (operator-set weight), (b) recency (exponential decay over recorded_at age), (c) trust (per-source provenance score), (d) outcome-tuned weight (from MT-158), (e) embedding-similarity to query. Formula version exposed as constant `INJECTION_SCORING_FORMULA_VERSION` consumed by RetrievalPolicy.scoring_formula_version (MT-141). ProgressiveRetriever: `pub fn retrieve_progressive(query: &str, top_k: u32, tier: DegradationTier, load_signal: &dyn LoadSignal) -> Result<(Vec<RetrievedItem>, DegradationReport), RetrievalError>`. DegradationTier already defined in MT-142 as `{ Strict, Tiered, Aggressive }`. Tiered pipeline: full-text search -> vector search -> graph expansion -> re-rank; under load_signal pressure, skip latest tiers. DegradationReport: `{ tiers_completed: Vec<String>, tiers_skipped: Vec<String>, load_signal_at_start, total_duration_ms }`. LoadSignal trait: `pub trait LoadSignal { fn current_pressure() -> f64 (0.0..=1.0); }` so production wires to actual CPU/GPU metrics and tests inject deterministic values. Tests: scoring formula is deterministic for fixed inputs; formula version constant stable; progressive retriever completes all tiers under low load; skips graph + re-rank under high load; DegradationReport accurately tracks skipped tiers; never returns empty result when at least one tier succeeded.
risk_if_missed: Without graceful degradation FEMS fails hard under heavy load (parallel local models + browser + other apps), breaking the bounded-injection invariant.
validator_focus: Scoring formula is deterministic; LoadSignal trait is testable; DegradationReport accurately surfaces what was skipped.

minimum_controls: Scoring formula is deterministic; version constant pinned for reproducibility.
LoadSignal trait so tests inject deterministic pressure values; production wires real CPU/GPU metrics.
DegradationReport surfaces every skipped tier so the operator can diagnose degraded calls.
